heading: Sale of crude helium
label: 167d Sale of crude helium
notes: Amendments2013— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to sale of helium.Pub. L. 113–401996—Subsec. (a). , substituted “from persons who have entered into enforceable contracts to purchase an equivalent amount of crude helium from the Secretary” for “from the Secretary”.Pub. L. 104–273, § 4(a)Subsec. (b). , inserted “crude” before “helium” and inserted at end “Except as may be required by reason of subsection (a), sales of crude helium under this section shall be in amounts as the Secretary determines, in consultation with the helium industry, necessary to carry out this subsection with minimum market disruption.”Pub. L. 104–273, § 4(b)Subsec. (c). , which directed the amendment of subsec. (c) by substituting “all funds required to be repaid to the United States as of under this section (referred to in this subsection as ‘repayable amounts’). The price at which crude helium is sold by the Secretary shall not be less than the amount determined by the Secretary by—” and pars. (1) and (2) for “together with interest as provided in this subsection” and all that followed through the end of the subsec., was executed by making the substitution for language which read “together with interest as provided in subsection (d) of this section, the following:” along with former pars. (1) to (3), to reflect the probable intent of Congress. Prior to amendment, pars. (1) to (3) read as follows:Pub. L. 104–273, § 4(c)(2)October 1, 1995“(1) Within twenty-five years from , the net capital and retained earnings of the helium production fund (established under prior to amendment by the Helium Act Amendments of 1960), determined by the Secretary as of , plus any moneys expended thereafter by the Department of the Interior from funds provided in the Supplemental Appropriation Act, 1959, for construction of a helium plant at Keyes, Oklahoma;September 13, 1960September 13, 1960section 164 of this title“(2) Within twenty-five years from the date of borrowing, all funds borrowed, as provided in section 167j of this chapter, to acquire and construct helium plants and facilities; and“(3) Within twenty-five years from , unless the Secretary determines that said period should be extended for not more than ten years, all funds borrowed, as provided in for all purposes other than those specified in clause (2) above.”September 13, 1960section 167j of this title, inserted “crude” after “Sales of”.Pub. L. 104–273, § 4(c)(1)Subsec. (d). , inserted heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Compound interest on the amounts specified in clauses (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (c) which have not been paid to the Treasury shall be calculated annually at rates determined by the Secretary of the Treasury taking into consideration the current average market yields of outstanding marketable obligations of the United States having maturities comparable to the investments authorized by this chapter, except that the interest rate on the amounts specified in clause (1) of subsection (c) shall be determined as of , and the interest rate on the obligations specified in clauses (2) and (3) of subsection (c) as of the time of each borrowing.”Pub. L. 104–273, § 4(d)Sept. 13, 1960Subsecs. (e), (f). , (f), redesignated subsec. (f) as (e)(1), added par. (2), and struck out former subsec. (e) which read as follows: “Helium shall be sold for medical purposes at prices which will permit its general use therefor; and all sales of helium to non-Federal purchasers shall be upon condition that the Federal Government shall have a right to repurchase helium so sold that has not been lost or dissipated, when needed for Government use, under terms and at prices established by regulations.”Pub. L. 104–273, § 4(e)

Transfer of FunctionsAtomic Energy Commission abolished and functions transferred by sections 5814 and 5841 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. See also Transfer of Functions notes set out under those sections.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall offer crude helium for sale in such quantities, at such times, at not less than the minimum price established under subsection (b)(7), and under such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out this subsection with minimum market disruption.

children.heading: Federal purchases
children.label: 2 Federal purchases
children.text: Federal users may purchase refined helium with priority pipeline access under this subsection from persons who have entered into enforceable contracts to purchase an equivalent quantity of crude helium at the in-kind price from the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: to maximize total recovery of helium from the Federal Helium Reserve over the long term;

children.label: B
children.text: to maximize the total financial return to the taxpayer;

children.label: C
children.text: to manage crude helium sales according to the ability of the Secretary to extract and produce helium from the Federal Helium Reserve;

children.label: D
children.text: to give priority to meeting the helium demand of Federal users in the event of any disruption to the Federal Helium Reserve; and

children.label: E
children.text: to carry out this subsection with minimum market disruption.

children.label: A
children.text: for fiscal year 2015, 10 percent of the total volume of crude helium made available for that fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2016 through 2019, a percentage of the total volume of crude helium that is 15 percentage points greater than the percentage made available for the previous fiscal year; and

children.label: C
children.text: for fiscal year 2020 and each fiscal year thereafter, 100 percent of the total volume of crude helium made available for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Federal purchases
children.label: 3 Federal purchases
children.text: Federal users may purchase refined helium with priority pipeline access under this subsection from persons who have entered into enforceable contracts to purchase an equivalent quantity of crude helium at the in-kind price from the Secretary.

children.heading: Auction format
children.label: 6 Auction format
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct each auction using a method that maximizes revenue to the Federal Government.

children.label: A
children.text: The sale price of crude helium in auctions held by the Secretary under paragraph (2).

children.label: B
children.text: Price recommendations and disaggregated data from a qualified, independent third party who has no conflict of interest, who shall conduct a confidential survey of qualifying domestic helium transactions.

children.label: C
children.text: The volume-weighted average price of all crude helium and pure helium purchased, sold, or processed by persons in all qualifying domestic helium transactions.

children.label: D
children.text: The volume-weighted average cost of converting gaseous crude helium into pure helium.

children.label: i
children.text: the volumes and associated prices in dollars per thousand cubic feet of all crude and pure helium purchased, sold, or processed by persons in qualifying domestic helium transactions;

children.label: ii
children.text: the volumes and associated costs in dollars per thousand cubic feet of converting crude helium into pure helium; and

children.label: iii
children.text: refinery capacity and future capacity estimates.

children.label: i
children.text: any person prevailing in auctions under paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: any person that has acquired crude helium from the Secretary from the Federal Helium Reserve by means other than an auction under paragraph (2) after , including nonallocated sales.October 2, 2013

children.label: A
children.text: to approximate crude helium prices; and

children.label: B
children.text: to ensure the recovery of fair value for the taxpayers of the United States from sales of crude helium.

children.heading: Protection of confidentiality
children.label: 10 Protection of confidentiality
children.text: The Secretary shall adopt such administrative policies and procedures as the Secretary considers necessary and reasonable to ensure the confidentiality of information submitted pursuant to this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (4)(A), the Secretary shall hold a one-time sale of helium, no later than from amounts available in fiscal year 2016 pursuant to this section. Full and final payment for the sale must be made no later than 45 days after the date the sale takes place.August 1, 2014

children.heading: Phase C: continued access for Federal users
children.label: c Phase C: continued access for Federal users

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall offer crude helium for sale to Federal users in such quantities, at such times, at such prices required to reimburse the Secretary for the full costs of the sales, and under such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Federal purchases
children.label: 2 Federal purchases
children.text: Federal users may purchase refined helium with priority pipeline access under this subsection from persons who have entered into enforceable contracts to purchase an equivalent quantity of crude helium at the in-kind price from the Secretary.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 3 Effective date
children.text: This subsection applies beginning on the day after the date described in subsection (b)(4)(B).

children.heading: Phase D: disposal of assets
children.label: d Phase D: disposal of assets

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not earlier than 2 years after the date of commencement of Phase C described in subsection (c) and not later than , the Secretary shall designate as excess property and dispose of all facilities, equipment, and other real and personal property, and all interests in the same, held by the United States in the Federal Helium System.September 30, 2021

children.heading: Applicable law
children.label: 2 Applicable law
children.text: The disposal of the property described in paragraph (1) shall be in accordance with subtitle I of title 40.

children.heading: Proceeds
children.label: 3 Proceeds
children.text: All proceeds accruing to the United States by reason of the sale or other disposal of the property described in paragraph (1) shall be treated as funds received under this chapter for purposes of subsection (e).

children.heading: Costs
children.label: 4 Costs
children.text: All costs associated with the sale and disposal (including costs associated with termination of personnel) and with the cessation of activities under this subsection shall be paid from amounts available in the Helium Production Fund established under subsection (e).

children.label: A
children.text: well head maintenance at the Cliffside Field;

children.label: B
children.text: capital investments in maintenance and upgrades of facilities that pressurize the Cliffside Field;

children.label: C
children.text: capital investments in maintenance and upgrades of equipment related to the storage, withdrawal, enrichment, transportation, purification, and sale of crude helium from the Federal Helium Reserve;

children.label: D
children.text: entering into purchase, lease, or other agreements to drill new or uncap existing wells to maximize the recovery of crude helium from the Federal Helium System; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other scheduled or unscheduled maintenance of the Federal Helium System.

children.heading: Excess funds
children.label: 2 Excess funds
children.text: Amounts in the Helium Production Fund in excess of amounts the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out paragraph (1) shall be paid to the general fund of the Treasury and used to reduce the annual Federal budget deficit.

children.heading: Retirement of public debt
children.label: 3 Retirement of public debt
children.text: Out of amounts paid to the general fund of the Treasury under paragraph (2), the Secretary of the Treasury shall use $51,000,000 to retire public debt.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 4 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary of the Interior shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives a report describing all expenditures by the Bureau of Land Management to carry out this chapter.October 2, 2013
